meta.title: Video to MP3 — Free Audio Extractor, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Turn any video into an .mp3 right in your browser — MP4, MOV, MKV, WebM, AVI and more. Nothing is uploaded; your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Video to MP3 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Extract the audio from any video into .mp3, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Video to MP3 — extract the audio in your browser
hero.lead: Drop any video — MP4, MOV, MKV, WebM, AVI — and pull out just the audio as an .mp3. It's extracted on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.h2: Turn any video into an MP3
prose.p1: Sometimes you only need the sound from a video: the music, an interview, a lecture, a voice note, the audio of a livestream you recorded. Extracting to MP3 drops the picture entirely and keeps just the audio in a small file that plays in every music app, podcast player and car stereo.
prose.p2: Most online extractors upload your whole video to a server first — slow for big files, and it hands your footage to a stranger. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't: the audio is extracted locally in your browser, so nothing is stored or logged and even a long recording starts instantly with no upload.
faq.q1: Which video formats can I use?
faq.a1: Most common ones — MP4, MOV, MKV, WebM and AVI, among others. Whatever your browser can read is decoded locally and the audio is written out as a standard .mp3.
faq.a2: No. The extraction happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q3: What MP3 quality do I get?
faq.a3: You choose the bitrate before converting — from 128 kbps (small) up to 320 kbps (best quality). 192 kbps is a good default for speech and most music.
